metaTitle: The Boeing Atmosphere Global Documentation Hub - Components - Checkbox

header.title: Checkbox
header.description: Checkboxes let users select one or more items from a list, or turn an item on or off

items: Use Checkboxes if multiple options can be selected from a list
Selected items are more prominent than unselected items
Checkboxes can be used within other components such as tables and lists

data.resource: Figma: Atmosphere Foundations and Components Library

sections.textBefore:
States

Checkboxes appear in five different states to effectively communicate the status of the component.

sections.text:
Measurements

The checkbox comes in two density modes: relaxed and condensed.

sections.text:
Usage

  • Use checkboxes for selecting multiple options from a list.
  • They can be included in lists that have subsections.
  • The checkbox component comes in two types: interactive non-interactive.
  • Interactive checkboxes are used as standalone items or inside non-interactive list components.
  • Non-interactive checkboxes are used when the parent container is interactive, such as an interactive list component.
  • Use checkboxes instead of switches for turning an item on or off in a desktop environment.
  • Use checkboxes instead of switches when multiple related options can be selected from a list. They are more effective in grouping similar items and take up less space than switches.

Label

By default, the checkbox component includes a text label.

  • Use the default text label to give a short, meaningful description of the checkbox when it's used alone in a layout.
  • Turn off the default text label when the checkbox is part of another component. For example, in a list component, the checkbox description is provided by the list header label.

images.text: Checkbox description is provided by the list header label in the list component.
Avoid using the checkbox’s default text label when used inside a list component

sections.text:
Behavior

Multiple checkboxes in a list can be selected.

Checkboxes can have a parent-child relationship with other checkboxes.

  • When you check a parent checkbox, all its child checkboxes are also checked.
  • When you uncheck a parent checkbox, all its child checkboxes are also unchecked.
  • If only some of the child checkboxes are checked, the parent checkbox will show as partially checked (indeterminate). checking this indeterminate checkbox will then check all child checkboxes.

Use cases

Users should be able to:

  • Navigate to a checkbox with assistive technology.
  • Toggle the checkbox on and off.
  • Select either the text label or the checkbox to select an option.

Keyboard

Users should be able to navigate to and select an active item using the specified keys:

data.actions: To move to the next element in a group
Toggles a focused checkbox between selected and unselected

sections.text:
Initial focus

When in groups: Tab moves focus into the parent checkbox as it’s the first interactive element of the group.

Pressing the tab key again moves the focus to the first child checkbox of the group.

Labeling elements

The accessibility label for an individual checkbox is typically the same as the adjacent text label.

If the UI text is correctly linked to the checkbox, assistive tech (such as a screenreader) will read the UI text followed by the component’s role.
